title: The memory market suffers a price crisis: SK Hynix's CEO calls them abnormally high, Nvidia delays its RTX 50 Super due to the cost of GDDR7, and Valve warns that the situation will worsen.
summary: Semiconductor memory prices are at exceptionally high levels, affecting the launch of new GPUs and the storage supply chain.

title: Artificial intelligence faces growing skepticism: X-ray chatbots are dangerously confident, euphoria deflates, and tech stocks fall.
summary: Various reports question the excessive enthusiasm for AI, pointing out technical limitations, stock market falls, and negative effects on global decision-making.

title: A mod allows playing GTA III and Vice City inside GTA: San Andreas, both on PC and on a TV in the game.
summary: The modding community has managed to run classic Grand Theft Auto titles within the San Andreas engine, reviving iconic cities.

title: AMD Ryzen 7 7700X3D drops to $279 two days after its launch, becoming the king of the mid-range.
summary: AMD's X3D processor has suffered a quick price reduction, making it extremely competitive in the mid-range market.

title: A video game development contest limits game size to 1.44 MB in homage to the floppy disk.
summary: A Korean organization holds a contest in which participants must create a complete game that fits on a single 3.5-inch floppy disk.

title: A mod attaches 11 fans and an AIO to an RTX 3080, reducing temperature by up to 33°C but barely gaining 4 FPS.
summary: The extreme cooling of a GPU with multiple fans and a liquid system manages to drastically lower temperatures, but the performance increase is insignificant and the noise, deafening.

title: Kimi, the Chinese artificial intelligence model, generates debate about its potential and risks.
summary: Articles from TechCrunch and Hacker News analyze the impact and implications of the AI assistant Kimi on the technological landscape.

title: US and China intensify their control strategies over artificial intelligence: new regulations, bodies, and military approaches.
summary: Both Washington and Beijing are taking steps to dictate AI development, whether through restrictions, multilateral bodies, or defense doctrines.

title: Protests against artificial intelligence data centers spread across the United States.
summary: Demonstrations in 37 states protest the environmental and social impact of the massive AI infrastructures.

title: Spotify removes 75 million songs generated by artificial intelligence and plans to continue doing so.
summary: The streaming platform removes a huge number of tracks created by bots to combat fraudulent content.

title: San Francisco demands that Apple and Google remove AI 'undressing' apps from their stores.
summary: The city urges tech giants to remove apps that use artificial intelligence to create non-consensual sexual images.

title: Netflix paid $587 million for Ben Affleck's artificial intelligence startup.
summary: The streaming company acquires InterPositive, founded by the actor, to integrate AI into its productions.

title: Artificial intelligence assigns harsher incarceration conditions to black prisoners in Ontario.
summary: A report reveals that an AI system used in Canadian prisons racially discriminates against inmates.

title: The FCC chairman accepted $63,000 worth of gifts from Paramount while the company needed approvals.
summary: Brendan Carr received tickets and other gifts from CBS/Paramount during key regulatory processes.

title: ICE shared Medicaid data it should not have had with the company Palantir.
summary: The US immigration agency transferred confidential medical information to the data analytics firm.

title: The rise of artificial intelligence in Australia is not what it seems: spending on data centers distorts the economy.
summary: Investment in AI infrastructure hides a weakness in the rest of the economy and barely contributes to GDP.

title: Waymo resumes its self-driving car service in San Francisco after a one-hour pause.
summary: The Alphabet company restored operation of its driverless taxis after a brief interruption.

title: All electric vehicles that were discontinued in the United States this year.
summary: A compilation by TechCrunch lists the EV models that ceased production or sales in the US market.

title: Apple Music and Apple One raise their subscription prices due to licensing costs.
summary: The music platform and service bundle increase their monthly fees for users.

title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang's leather jacket is auctioned for nearly a million dollars.
summary: The iconic garment of the semiconductor company's leader was sold at a charity auction well above its estimated value.
